stories.headline: Cysecurity Firms Dismantle Glassworm Botnet in Coordinated Strike
stories.summary: A major cybersecurity operation successfully took down the Glassworm botnet, which had infected developers through poisoned tools and packages. The coordinated effort by CrowdStrike, Google, and Shadowserver Foundation eliminated all four command-and-control channels simultaneously.
stories.original_title: How cybersecurity firms took down Glassworm botnet in one shot
stories.context: Imagine a "botnet" as a massive army of infected computers that hackers control remotely without the owners knowing, like puppets on strings. The Glassworm group was a notorious hacker team that used this army to steal secrets and money by hiding inside software tools that developers use to build other programs. For years, these hackers operated in the shadows, but a global coalition of security experts finally tracked down their hidden command centers. By shutting down these central control points simultaneously, they effectively cut the strings, leaving the infected computers harmless and breaking the hackers' grip.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a big win for internet safety. It stops hackers from using thousands of infected computers to cause trouble, like stealing data or slowing down websites. It shows that working together can fix major digital problems.
stories.other_side: Some people worry that these attacks are just getting smarter. Even if one group is stopped, hackers might just create a new version to try again later.

stories.headline: US Special Ops Embraces 'Geeks with Guns' as AI Reshapes Warfare
stories.summary: US special operations leaders are redefining recruitment to include troops with advanced technical skills alongside traditional combat toughness. This shift reflects the increasing integration of drones and artificial intelligence into modern military engagements.
stories.original_title: Special ops wants "geeks with guns" bringing both grit and tech skills into combat
stories.context: Traditionally, being a special forces soldier meant mastering physical combat, stealth, and survival skills in harsh environments. However, modern warfare now relies heavily on technology like drones and artificial intelligence to gather intelligence and strike targets from a distance. This shift means that military leaders are looking for recruits who are not just tough fighters but also skilled technicians who can understand and operate complex digital systems. They are essentially creating a new type of warrior who is just as comfortable coding or fixing a drone as they are handling a rifle.
stories.why_it_matters: The military is changing how it picks soldiers. They now want tech experts who can handle drones and AI, not just fighters. This means war will rely more on computers and code than just guns.
stories.other_side: Critics say this changes the soul of the military. They worry that focusing too much on tech might make soldiers less ready for real, physical combat on the ground.

stories.headline: The Witcher 3 Gets 'Songs of the Past' Expansion After a Decade
stories.summary: CD Projekt Red has officially announced a new expansion for The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt titled Songs of the Past, developed in collaboration with the original team. This marks a significant return for the iconic RPG series ten years after its last major DLC.
stories.original_title: Geralt's back for one last Roach ride in long-rumoured The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt expansion Songs of the Past
stories.context: The Witcher 3 is a famous video game series based on fantasy books, known for its deep story and open world where players make choices that change the outcome. After its huge success, the developers released several small add-ons called DLCs to extend the game, but fans have been waiting for a major new chapter for ten years. This new expansion, "Songs of the Past," brings back the original creative team to deliver a substantial new adventure rather than just a tiny update. It represents a rare and exciting return for a classic game franchise that has been dormant in terms of major content for a decade.
stories.why_it_matters: Gamers are very happy because their favorite game from ten years ago is getting new content. It proves that old games can still be popular and make money. It keeps the gaming community alive and excited.
stories.other_side: Some fans think this is just a cash grab. They argue that making money off an old game without releasing a brand-new sequel feels lazy to them.

stories.headline: Allot Ltd. Showcases Security-as-a-Service at William Blair Conference
stories.summary: Allot Ltd., a global provider of network intelligence and security solutions, is set to present at the 46th Annual William Blair Growth Stock Conference. The company will highlight its innovative Security-as-a-Service offerings for communications providers and enterprises.
stories.original_title: Allot to Meet Investors at the 46th Annual William Blair Growth Stock Conference on June 2, 2026
stories.context: Allot Ltd. is a company that builds technology to help internet service providers manage network traffic and keep it secure from cyber threats. They are presenting at a major financial conference to show investors how their new "Security-as-a-Service" model works, which is essentially renting out high-level security protection instead of selling physical hardware. This approach allows companies to pay for security updates and monitoring as a subscription service, making it easier for them to stay safe without needing huge upfront costs. The presentation aims to prove that this flexible, cloud-based security model is the future for protecting large networks.
stories.why_it_matters: This company is trying to sell security as a subscription service. It means businesses don't have to buy expensive software; they just pay a fee for protection. This makes high-level security easier for smaller companies to get.
stories.other_side: People are worried about trusting outside companies with their data. If the service provider gets hacked, all their clients' information could be lost at once.

stories.headline: Streamex Launches Decentralized Liquidity Infrastructure for Tokenized Securities
stories.summary: Streamex Corp. has launched a 24/7 decentralized secondary liquidity infrastructure designed for tokenized real-world assets. GLDY serves as the inaugural asset on this new platform, marking a significant step in the tokenization ecosystem.
stories.original_title: Streamex Corp. and Orca Announce Launch of 24/7 Decentralized Secondary Liquidity Infrastructure for Tokenized Securities; GLDY the Inaugural Asset
stories.context: Tokenization is the process of turning real-world assets, like stocks or real estate, into digital tokens on a blockchain, which is a secure, shared digital ledger. Streamex Corp. has created a platform that allows these digital tokens to be bought and sold easily at any time, solving the problem that many such assets are usually hard to trade quickly. Their first asset on this platform, called GLDY, serves as a test case to show how smooth and accessible this new trading system can be. This infrastructure helps bridge the gap between traditional finance and the fast-moving world of cryptocurrency trading.
stories.why_it_matters: This lets people buy and sell parts of real-world items, like buildings or art, using digital coins. It makes investing in big things easier and faster for regular people. It could change how we buy property forever.
stories.other_side: Experts say this is very risky and confusing. The rules are not clear yet, so people might lose their money if the system breaks or if they get scammed.

stories.headline: Lenovo Releases Critical Intel UHD Graphics Driver Update for Windows 10
stories.summary: Lenovo has issued a new Intel UHD Graphics Driver package (version 26.20.100.7986) compatible with various Windows 10 versions. This update aims to fix existing issues, add new functions, and upgrade system performance for users relying on integrated graphics.
stories.original_title: Lenovo Intel UHD Graphics Driver 26.20.100.7986 for Windows 10 Fall Creators 64-bit
stories.context: Graphics drivers are small pieces of software that act as translators between your computer's operating system and its graphics card, ensuring images display correctly on your screen. Lenovo issued an update for Intel UHD Graphics drivers because older versions had bugs that caused visual glitches or slowed down performance on Windows 10 computers. By installing this new version, users can fix these errors and potentially see smoother performance when watching videos or doing light graphic work. It is a routine maintenance update designed to keep everyday computer tasks running smoothly without crashing.
stories.why_it_matters: This update fixes bugs that make computers run slowly or look weird. It helps people who use older Windows 10 systems keep their screens working properly. It is a simple but important fix for daily computer use.
stories.other_side: Some users hate forced updates. They worry that new changes might break other programs they use or slow down their older laptops even more.

stories.headline: NVIDIA Unveils New Studio Drivers for Creative Professionals
stories.summary: NVIDIA has released version 610.47 of its Studio Drivers, optimized for artists, creators, and 3D developers. These drivers undergo extensive testing to ensure maximum reliability and performance across multi-app creative workflows.
stories.context: NVIDIA makes powerful graphics cards that are essential not just for gaming, but also for professional work like 3D modeling, video editing, and animation. Their "Studio Drivers" are specially tested versions of their software that prioritize stability and reliability over raw speed, which is crucial for professionals who cannot afford crashes during important projects. Version 610.47 was released to ensure that artists can work across multiple applications simultaneously without technical issues interfering with their workflow. This update ensures that creative tools run efficiently, allowing professionals to focus on their art rather than troubleshooting their computers.
stories.why_it_matters: Artists and video editors get better tools to do their jobs faster. These updates help their computers handle heavy tasks without crashing. It supports the creative industry by making software more reliable.
stories.other_side: Regular gamers might not care about this. They prefer drivers made for playing games, and these studio updates might not help with speed in video games at all.

stories.headline: NVIDIA Updates GeForce RTX 3060 Ti Drivers for Windows 10
stories.summary: NVIDIA has provided updated installation files for the GeForce RTX 3060 Ti graphics driver (version 27.21.14.6089) for Windows 10 systems. Users can overwrite-install this package to resolve issues or enhance gaming capabilities.
stories.original_title: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti Graphics Driver 27.21.14.6089 for Windows 10 Vibranium 64-bit
stories.context: The GeForce RTX 3060 Ti is a popular graphics card used primarily for playing modern video games at high settings. NVIDIA released a specific driver update for this card to fix known bugs that might cause games to freeze or look incorrect, as well as to improve overall gaming performance. Users can install this update to overwrite their current driver, which often results in smoother gameplay and better frame rates. It is a targeted fix designed to enhance the experience for gamers who rely on this specific hardware for entertainment.
stories.why_it_matters: This helps gamers play their favorite games without glitches. It fixes errors that might crash the game or make graphics look bad. It keeps the gaming experience smooth and fun for players.
stories.other_side: Some people feel like they have to constantly update their drivers. It can be annoying to keep checking for new versions just to keep things working right.

stories.headline: Brain Overwork Linked to Always-On Digital Lifestyle
stories.summary: Neurologists warn that the modern 'always-on' digital culture prevents the brain from achieving true rest, leading to chronic overwork. The loss of natural silence and idle moments in daily life is cited as a key factor in this cognitive strain.
stories.original_title: Even while resting, the brain may still be overworked, neurologists warn about today’s “always-on” lifestyle — There was a time when silence came naturally. Long train rides meant staring out of the window. Waiting in a queue meant observing people around. Nights ended with conversations or quiet thoughts before sleep. Today, those empty spaces rarely exist. Every pau…
stories.context: Neurologists are scientists who study the brain and nervous system, and they are warning that our constant connection to smartphones and the internet prevents our brains from truly resting. In the past, humans had natural moments of boredom or silence that allowed the brain to process information and recover, but today's "always-on" culture eliminates these breaks. This constant stimulation keeps the brain in a state of chronic overwork, similar to how muscles get tired if never allowed to rest. The loss of idle time is linked to increased stress and cognitive strain, suggesting we need to consciously disconnect to maintain mental health.
stories.why_it_matters: Our brains are getting tired because we never stop looking at screens. This constant connection stops us from truly relaxing. It explains why so many people feel stressed and burned out today.
stories.other_side: Some argue that technology also helps us relax. Apps for meditation or easy access to entertainment can actually reduce stress if used in moderation.

stories.headline: Q2 Metals Kicks Off Major Lithium Exploration Program
stories.summary: Q2 Metals has initiated its largest field program to date at the Cisco Lithium Project, targeting approximately 20,000 metres of drilling. The summer exploration focuses on infill drilling to convert inferred resources into higher-confidence reserves.
stories.original_title: Q2 Metals Announces 2026 Summer Exploration Program and Project Development Plans for the Cisco Lithium Project — Highlights: The largest field program to date at the Cisco Lithium Project has started, with the summer program targeting approximately 20,000 metres of drilling: Priority is on infill drilling of the main Cisco deposit area to convert existing Inferred resou…
stories.context: Lithium is a critical metal used to make batteries for electric cars and smartphones, driving a huge global demand for mining exploration. Q2 Metals has started a large drilling program at the Cisco Lithium Project to find out exactly how much lithium is underground and how easy it is to extract. They are using "infill drilling," which means drilling closer together in areas where they already suspect lithium exists, to turn rough estimates into confirmed, valuable reserves. This step is crucial for proving that the site is economically viable before investing billions into building a mine.
stories.why_it_matters: Lithium is needed for electric car batteries. Finding more of it helps us move away from gas cars. This search is key to building a cleaner future for transportation.
stories.other_side: Mining lithium hurts the environment. It uses a lot of water and can damage local ecosystems, which makes some people oppose the drilling projects.
